formula_note: Low = 1,204 + 559 (later HLC ground-conflict series only). Best = 1,125 + 346 + 142 + 174 + 27 (older HLC detailed Kosovo total). High = 1,204 + 559 + 174 + 27 (later HLC ground-conflict series plus older HLC NATO-caused military deaths in Kosovo).
uncertainty_note: Main uncertainty is the disagreement between HLC 2012 and HLC 2023 on KLA and VJ/MoI battlefield deaths. NATO-caused military deaths inside Kosovo are only explicit in the 2012 note.
quality_note: Registry-based but affected by competing HLC update states.

formula_note: Low = 6,200 + 189 + 220, treating the 2012 'more than 6,200 killed or forcibly disappeared' line as a conservative lower-bound anchor for Serbian-force victims. Best = 6,872 + 189 + 220. High = 6,872 + 328 + 136 + 220 + 46, summing the strongest side-specific direct-killing components available.
uncertainty_note: The main dispute is whether to use the older HLC 2012 non-Albanian civilian total (189 killed in Kosovo, 46 by NATO) or the later HLC 2023 KLA-attributed total of 464 civilians. I do not average them mechanically; instead I use them as central-versus-upper scenarios.
quality_note: Strong attacker-specific series for Albanian civilians; weaker reconciliation for non-Albanian civilian victims.

formula_note: No separate published unknown-status direct-death series was identified in the public high-level HLC/KMB summaries for this episode.
uncertainty_note: Core DR uses strict exclusion of unknown-status deaths. This almost certainly omits some edge cases, but the available named-victim summaries classify most victims as civilian or armed-group members.
quality_note: Unknown-status row left null rather than imputed.

formula_note: 79-day episode converted to months at 79/30.4375 = 2.5955 months. Low force stock = 30,000 Priština Corps + 15,000 police + 10,000 active KLA subset = 55,000. Best = 35,000 Priština Corps + 15,000 police + 15,000 KLA = 65,000. High = 35,000 Priština Corps + 15,000 police + 15,000 other soldiers in Kosovo + 18,000 KLA = 83,000.
uncertainty_note: This excludes off-theater NATO aircrew and sorts KLA strength into active-fighter subsets because the ICTY judgment says not all 17,000-18,000 were full-time soldiers. Including theater-assigned NATO aircrew would lower DR.
quality_note: Grounded in ICTY force-structure evidence, but still scenario-based.

formula_note: Low = average of 1,800,000 and (1,800,000 - 862,979), multiplied by 2.5955 months = 3,551,945 person-months. Best = average of 2,044,000 and (2,044,000 - 862,979), multiplied by 2.5955 = 4,185,243. High = average of 2,188,817 and (2,188,817 - 862,979), multiplied by 2.5955 = 4,561,113.
uncertainty_note: This treats civilians physically present in Kosovo as declining roughly linearly from a start stock to a June 9 stock after refugee outflow. Internal IDPs stay in the denominator. The biggest uncertainty is whether the population anchors are closer to de jure or de facto stocks by March 1999.
quality_note: Scenario-based but grounded in explicit population and displacement anchors.

formula_note: DR_v1_direct = (mil_deaths_direct / mil_person_months) / (civ_deaths_direct / civ_person_months), with strict exclusion of unknown-status deaths.
uncertainty_note: Best estimate uses 1,814 military direct deaths, 7,281 civilian direct deaths, 168,706 military person-months, and 4,185,243 civilian person-months. The range widens mainly because of non-Albanian civilian series disagreement and because NATO aircrew are excluded from the military denominator.
quality_note: Useful episode-level DR, but not precise enough for municipality-level inference.

uncertainty_note: Attacker-specific direct-death component from later HLC summary.
Kosovo-only NATO-caused Albanian civilian deaths are explicit here, though later HLC bombing summaries emphasize all-FRY totals.

victim_side: Serbs and other non-Kosovo Albanian civilians

uncertainty_note: Held as an upper-scenario component because it exceeds the older HLC 2012 total for all non-Albanian civilians killed in Kosovo.
quality_note: Attacker attribution strong, episode geography slightly less explicit in the 2023 sentence.

uncertainty_note: Held as an upper-scenario component only.
quality_note: Attacker attribution strong, episode geography slightly less explicit in the 2023 sentence.

uncertainty_note: Later HLC series; older HLC note gives 1,125, so this is not the only plausible coding state.
Later HLC series; older detailed HLC note gives 488 (346 soldiers + 142 police) in ground clashes.
